sections.title: Regulator's role as conciliator in complaint resolution
sections.summary: This section outlines the regulator's responsibilities when acting as a conciliator during complaint resolution. The regulator must notify the data subject and responsible party of their decision to act as conciliator, the date, time, and location of conciliation meetings, and ensure timely communication of relevant documents. The regulator may consolidate related complaints, notify attendees, request documents, and conduct meetings electronically or in person. If no conciliation meeting occurs, the regulator must set an alternative date and notify attendees. After conciliation, the regulator issues a conciliation certificate within a reasonable time.

requirements.text: Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party of decision to act as conciliator
Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party of date, time, and location of conciliation meeting
Regulator may consolidate related complaints to handle in the same conciliation process
Regulator must ensure attendees are notified within a reasonable time of meeting date
Regulator may request all relevant documents from data subject and responsible party
Regulator may conduct meetings electronically or through other means as appropriate
Regulator must set alternative date if no conciliation meeting occurs
Regulator must issue conciliation certificate within reasonable time after meeting

sections.source_quote: Indien die Reguleerder tydens die ondersoek van ‟n klagte besluit om ingevolge artikel 76(1)(b) van die Wet as ‟n konsiliator op te tree, en ‟n konsiliasievergadering saamroep, moet die Reguleerder, so gou as prakties moontlik, die datasubjek en die geïmpliseerde verantwoordelike party op Vorm 6 oor die volgende in kennis stel: (a) die Reguleerder se besluit om as ‟n konsiliator in die aangeleentheid op te tree; en (b) die datum, tyd en plek van die konsiliasievergadering.
sections.relevance_reason: Regulator's conciliation process in complaint resolution

sections.heading: (1) If the Regulator intends to investigate any matter contemplated in Chapter 10
sections.title: Notification of investigation intentions to parties
sections.summary: This section requires the regulator to notify relevant parties before initiating any investigation under Chapter 10 of the POPIA. The regulator must inform the responsible party of the complaint or subject of investigation and provide them with a reasonable time to submit written responses. This applies to all parties involved in data protection investigations.

requirements.text: Regulator must notify parties of intended investigations before conducting them
Regulator must inform responsible party of complaint or subject of investigation
Regulator must provide responsible party with reasonable time to submit written response

sections.source_quote: Indien die Reguleerder voornemens is om enige aangeleentheid beoog in Hoofstuk 10 van die Wet beoog, te ondersoek, moet die Reguleerder ingevolge artikel 79 van die Wet die partye op wie die ondersoek betrekking het, op Deel I van Vorm 8 verwittig van sodanige voorneme voordat die ondersoek gedoen word.
sections.relevance_reason: regulator's investigation process and party notification

sections.heading: (1) If it appears from a complaint or any written reply to the complaint under
sections.title: Mediation for complaint resolution
sections.summary: This section allows the regulator to mediate between parties to reach a settlement or binding agreement under the POPIA. The regulator may propose mediation to parties to resolve complaints, and if a mediation meeting is arranged, must notify the data subject and responsible party of the date, time, and location. The regulator has the same powers as a conciliator in the mediation process and must issue a mediation certificate after the meeting. If no settlement is reached, the regulator proceeds with the complaint as per section 76 of the POPIA.

requirements.text: Regulator may mediate between parties to reach settlement or binding agreement
Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party of mediation meeting date, time, and location
Regulator has same powers as conciliator in mediation process
Regulator must issue mediation certificate within reasonable time after meeting

sections.source_quote: Indien dit uit ‟n klagte of enige skriftelike antwoord op die klagte kragtens artikel 79(b)(ii) van die Wet of tydens ‟n konsiliasievergadering, voorkom dat dit moontlik kan wees om ‟n skikking tussen die partye en indien gepas, bevredigende versekerings soos beoog in artikel 80 van die Wet, te verkry, kan die Reguleerder persoonlik oorleg pleeg met die partye, per elektroniese kommunikasiemiddele, of per enige ander middele soos gepas geag word om te poog om ‟n skikking te verkry en indien van toepassing, bevredigende versekerings soos beoog in artikel 80 van die Wet.
sections.relevance_reason: Regulator mediation process for complaint resolution

sections.title: Audit procedures for compliance
sections.summary: This section details the process for conducting audits under the POPIA. A request for an audit must be submitted to the regulator via Form 11 Part I. The regulator must notify the requester within a reasonable time if they decide to conduct an audit, and must provide the requester or responsible party with details of any decisions, actions, or opinions taken during the audit process. The regulator must determine the assessment period on a case-by-case basis.

requirements.text: Request for audit must be submitted to regulator via Form 11 Part I
Regulator must notify requester within reasonable time if audit is decided
Regulator must determine assessment period on a case-by-case basis
Regulator must inform requester or responsible party of decisions, actions, or opinions

sections.source_quote: „n Versoek om ‟n oudit ingevolge artikel 89(1) van die Wet moet op Deel 1 van Vorm 11 aan die Reguleerder voorgelê word.
sections.relevance_reason: Regulator audit procedures for compliance

sections.title: Notification of investigation developments
sections.summary: This section requires the regulator to notify the data subject and responsible party of developments during an investigation. The regulator must inform them of the progress of the investigation and the outcome of the investigation within a reasonable time. Specific notifications include when enforcement actions are not taken, when the data subject is referred to the enforcement committee, when enforcement actions are issued, when enforcement actions are withdrawn or amended, when appeals are filed, and when appeals are granted.

requirements.text: Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party of investigation developments
Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party of investigation outcome
Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party if enforcement actions are not taken
Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party if data subject is referred to enforcement committee
Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party if enforcement actions are issued
Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party if enforcement actions are withdrawn or amended
Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party if appeals are filed
Regulator must notify data subject and responsible party if appeals are granted

sections.source_quote: Deur die loop van ‟n ondersoek moet die Reguleerder binne ‟n redelike tyd vanaf die datum waarop ‟n besluit geneem of stap gedoen word- (a) die klaer, die datasubjek (indien nie die klaer nie) en die verantwoordelike party ingelig hou van die ontwikkelings in die ondersoek; en (b) die klaer, datasubjek (indien nie klaer nie) en die verantwoordelike party verwittig van die uitslag van die ondersoek.
sections.relevance_reason: regulator notifications on investigation outcomes

sections.heading: These Regulations shall be called the Regulations relating to the Protection of
sections.title: Regulations title and effective date
sections.summary: This section defines the POPIA Regulations as the regulations on the protection of personal information and states that they come into effect on a date specified by the regulator through proclamation in the State Gazette. The regulations are named after the Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (Act No. 4 of 2013).

requirements.text: The Regulations are titled 'Regulations on the protection of personal information'
The Regulations come into effect on a date specified by the regulator through proclamation in the State Gazette

sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates the timeframe for audit requests, which is critical for assessing the timeliness of data transfer compliance and audit processes.

requirements.text: Specify the date when awareness of audit requirement was first obtained
Explain any delays in requesting the audit

sections.source_quote: 6. TYDPERK
sections.relevance_reason: audit request delays and explanations

sections.heading: DATASUBJEK DEELNAME:
sections.title: Data subject participation in audit
sections.summary: This section details the data subject's rights to access personal information and correct information held by the data controller, which must be considered when conducting an audit.
sections.dtia_summary: This section addresses data subject participation in audits, which is relevant for assessing how data transfers impact individual rights and compliance.

requirements.text: Confirm data subject's right to access personal information
Confirm data subject's right to correct personal information

sections.source_quote: 7. DATASUBJEK DEELNAME:
sections.relevance_reason: Data subject rights to access and correct information

sections.heading: KENNISGEWING VAN 'N BESLUIT OOR 'N OUDIT
sections.title: Audit decision by regulator
sections.summary: The regulator has decided to conduct an audit of data processing activities under section 89(1) of the Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013. This decision is made by the Information Regulator based on the requirements of the Act.

requirements.text: Regulator decides to conduct an audit of data processing activities

sections.source_quote: Die Reguleerder het besluit om 'n oudit te doen ingevolge artikel 89(1) van die Wet op Beskerming van Persoonlike Inligting, 2013.
sections.relevance_reason: Regulator audit decision for data processing

sections.heading: INLIGTINGSVERWERKING WAT GEOUDIT MOET WORD
sections.title: Data processing requiring audit
sections.summary: This section specifies the data processing activities that require audit. The exact scope and details of the processing activities to be audited are left blank in the document.
sections.relevance_reason: Audit requirements for data processing

sections.heading: PERSONE GERAAK DEUR DIE TERSAAKLIKE INLIGTINGSVERWERKINGSPRAKTYK(E)
sections.title: Persons affected by the processing practice
sections.summary: This section identifies the persons affected by the processing practice. The specific individuals or entities impacted by the data processing activities are left blank in the document.
sections.relevance_reason: Identifies affected persons but leaves blank

sections.heading: WAAROM 'N OUDIT GEDOEN GAAN WORD/NIE GEDOEN GAAN WORD NIE
sections.title: Reasons for audit or no audit
sections.summary: This section outlines the reasons why an audit should or should not be conducted. The specific conditions and criteria for determining whether an audit is necessary are left blank in the document.
sections.relevance_reason: audit criteria for data compliance

sections.heading: SPESIFIEKE ASPEKTE VAN DIE VERWERKING VAN INLIGTING WAAROP DIE OUDIT MOET
sections.title: Specific aspects of data processing for audit
sections.summary: This section details the specific aspects of data processing that must be covered by the audit. The exact scope and details of the processing activities to be audited are left blank in the document.
sections.relevance_reason: audit scope for data processing

sections.heading: SPESIFIEKE ASPEKTE VAN DIE VERWERKING VAN INLIGTING WAAROP DIE OUDIT MOET (cont.)
